parameters.name: properties.hs_note_body

nodes.name: Calendly — New Booking
nodes.notes: SETUP: 1) Create a Calendly API credential in n8n using your Personal Access Token. 2) n8n will auto-register the webhook with Calendly when the workflow is activated.

nodes.name: Set — Contact ID
nodes.notes: Normalises the contact ID from either the Create or Update branch so downstream nodes always have a consistent $json.contactId reference regardless of which branch ran.

nodes.name: ClickUp — Create Prep Task
nodes.notes: SETUP: Replace YOUR_CLICKUP_LIST_ID with the numeric ID of the ClickUp list where prep tasks should land. Priority 2 = High. Due date is set to 2 hours before the meeting start time.

nodes.name: Gmail — Send Confirmation
nodes.notes: SETUP: Replace replyTo with your sales team's email address.

parameters.message:
={{ `Call Confirmed
📅

Your call is confirmed

We're looking forward to speaking with you, ${$('Format Booking Data').item.json.firstName}

Meeting details

📅 Date & time${$('Format Booking Data').item.json.startTimeFormatted}
📋 Session${$('Format Booking Data').item.json.eventTypeName}
⏱ Duration30 minutes
🎥 PlatformZoom
🎥  Join meeting

Before your call

✓  Check your inbox for a calendar invite with the meeting link

✓  Join 2–3 minutes early so we can start on time

✓  Have any questions or agenda items ready to share

Need to make changes?

📆 Reschedule✕ Cancel

Questions before the call? Simply reply to this email.
We'll get back to you as soon as possible.

` }}
parameters.subject: ={{ '✅ Confirmed: ' + $('Format Booking Data').item.json.eventTypeName + ' on ' + new Date($('Format Booking Data').item.json.startTime).toLocaleDateString('en-US', { weekday: 'long', month: 'long', day: 'numeric' }) }}

nodes.name: Slack — Notify Sales Channel
nodes.notes: SETUP: Replace YOUR_SLACK_CHANNEL_ID with your #sales or #inbound-leads channel ID.

parameters.text:
={{ ':phone: *New Sales Call Booked!*

' + ':bust_in_silhouette:  *Prospect:* ' + $('Format Booking Data').item.json.inviteeName + '
' + ':email:  *Email:* ' + $('Format Booking Data').item.json.inviteeEmail + '
' + ':office:  *Company:* ' + ($('Format Booking Data').item.json.inviteeOrg || '_Unknown — check prep task_') + '
' + ':calendar:  *Session:* ' + $('Format Booking Data').item.json.eventTypeName + '
' + ':clock3:  *Time:* ' + $('Format Booking Data').item.json.startTimeFormatted + '
' + ':video_camera:  *Join:* ' + $('Format Booking Data').item.json.meetingJoinUrl + '

' + ':white_check_mark: HubSpot deal created · ' + ':white_check_mark: ClickUp prep task created · ' + ':white_check_mark: Confirmation email sent' }}

parameters.name: properties.hs_next_step
parameters.value: =Complete pre-call research in ClickUp before the meeting

parameters.name: properties.description

nodes.name: Google Sheets — Log Booking
nodes.notes: SETUP: 1) Replace YOUR_GOOGLE_SHEET_ID with the ID from your spreadsheet URL (docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/SHEET_ID/edit). 2) Create a sheet tab named 'Bookings' with matching column headers in row 1. 3) Connect a Google Sheets OAuth2 credential.

nodes.name: Google Calendar — Create Rep Event
nodes.notes: SETUP: Connect your sales rep's Google Calendar OAuth2 credential. The event is created on their primary calendar with 60-min and 15-min popup reminders plus a 24-hour email reminder. Duration is hardcoded to 30 min — adjust the +30*60*1000 offset to match your actual event length.

additionalFields.summary: ={{ '📞 Sales Call: ' + $('Format Booking Data').item.json.inviteeName + ' (' + ($('Format Booking Data').item.json.inviteeOrg || 'Unknown Co.') + ')' }}
additionalFields.description:
={{ '🔗 Join URL: ' + $('Format Booking Data').item.json.meetingJoinUrl + '

' + '👤 Prospect: ' + $('Format Booking Data').item.json.inviteeName + '
' + '📧 Email: ' + $('Format Booking Data').item.json.inviteeEmail + '
' + '🏢 Company: ' + ($('Format Booking Data').item.json.inviteeOrg || 'TBD') + '
' + '📞 Phone: ' + ($('Format Booking Data').item.json.inviteePhone || 'TBD') + '

' + '📋 Event: ' + $('Format Booking Data').item.json.eventTypeName + '

' + '🔁 Reschedule: ' + $('Format Booking Data').item.json.rescheduleUrl + '
' + '❌ Cancel: ' + $('Format Booking Data').item.json.cancelUrl + '

' + '---
Auto-created by n8n Calendly workflow.' }}

nodes.name: Wait — Until 24h Before Meeting
nodes.notes: Pauses execution until exactly 24 hours before the scheduled meeting. Uses the reminder24hAt timestamp computed in Format Booking Data.

nodes.name: Gmail — Send 24h Reminder
nodes.notes: SETUP: Same Gmail OAuth credential as the confirmation email. Sent exactly 24 hours before the meeting time.

parameters.message:
={{ `24-Hour Reminder
⏰

Your call is tomorrow

Just a friendly heads-up, ${$('Format Booking Data').item.json.firstName}

Meeting details

📅 When${$('Format Booking Data').item.json.startTimeFormatted}
📋 Session${$('Format Booking Data').item.json.eventTypeName}
🎥  Save meeting link

Quick checklist

✓  Add the meeting link to your calendar

✓  Test your camera and microphone

✓  Prepare any questions you'd like to discuss

Need to make changes?

📆 Reschedule ✕ Cancel

Questions? Reply to this email — we're here to help.

` }}
parameters.subject: ={{ '⏰ Reminder: Your call is tomorrow — ' + $('Format Booking Data').item.json.eventTypeName }}

nodes.name: Wait — Until 1h Before Meeting
nodes.notes: Pauses execution until exactly 1 hour before the scheduled meeting.

nodes.name: Gmail — Send 1h Reminder
nodes.notes: SETUP: Same Gmail OAuth credential. Final reminder sent 1 hour before the call with a prominent join button.

parameters.message:
={{ `1-Hour Reminder
🚀

Your call starts in 1 hour

Almost time — we can't wait to connect, ${$('Format Booking Data').item.json.firstName}

🎥  Join meeting now

Your appointment

📅 When${$('Format Booking Data').item.json.startTimeFormatted}
📋 Session${$('Format Booking Data').item.json.eventTypeName}

Last-minute tips

✓  Join 2 minutes early to check your audio/video

✓  Find a quiet spot with good lighting

✓  Have your questions ready — we'll cover everything!

Still need to reschedule?

📆 Reschedule

See you very soon! Reply to this email if you need anything.

` }}
parameters.subject: ={{ '🚀 Starting in 1 hour: ' + $('Format Booking Data').item.json.eventTypeName }}

parameters.content:
## Calendly Sales Meeting Automation 
This workflow automatically manages a new meeting booking from start to finish. When a person books a meeting through Calendly, the workflow saves the booking details, updates customer records, creates tasks for the sales team, sends emails, and reminds the customer before the meeting. It helps the team stay organized and ensures no meeting is forgotten. Everything works automatically without manual work. The workflow also keeps all meeting information in one place so the sales team can easily prepare before the call.

### How it Works

	•	A customer books a meeting through Calendly.
	•	The workflow collects and organizes all booking details.
	•	The booking information is saved in Google Sheets.
	•	The workflow checks if the customer already exists in HubSpot.
	•	A customer profile and sales deal are created or updated automatically.
	•	A preparation task is created for the sales team in ClickUp.
	•	Confirmation and reminder emails are automatically sent to the customer before the meeting.

### Setup Steps

	1.	Connect your Calendly account to receive new booking details automatically.
	2.	Connect HubSpot so customer and deal information can be stored properly.
	3.	Connect Google Sheets and create a sheet to save booking records.
	4.	Connect Gmail for sending confirmation and reminder emails.
	5.	Connect Slack and ClickUp for team notifications and meeting preparation tasks.

## Step 1 : Receive and Prepare Booking Details

This step starts when a customer books a meeting in Calendly. The workflow collects the customer’s name, email, company, phone number, meeting time, and meeting links. After organizing the information, all booking details are saved into Google Sheets for record keeping. This helps the business keep a proper history of all scheduled meetings.

## Step 2 : Check Existing Customer

This step checks whether the customer already exists in HubSpot using their email address. The workflow searches the customer information and decides whether the record should be updated or a new one should be created. This helps avoid duplicate customer entries and keeps the system organized.

## Step 3 : Create or Update Customer Record

If the customer already exists, their information is updated with the latest booking details. If the customer is new, a new customer profile is created automatically in HubSpot. After that, the workflow stores the customer ID so the next steps can use the correct customer information easily.

## Step 4 : Create Calendar Event and Sales Deal

This step creates the meeting event in the sales representative’s Google Calendar. After that, a sales deal is created in HubSpot to track the meeting opportunity and customer progress. This helps the sales team stay prepared and manage upcoming meetings properly.

## Step 5 : Create Meeting Preparation Task

This step creates a preparation task for the sales team in ClickUp. The task includes customer details, meeting information, and a checklist to help the team prepare before the call. This makes it easier for the sales representative to research the customer and stay organized.

## Step 6 : Send Confirmation and Team Updates

This step sends a confirmation email to the customer after the booking is completed.
The email contains the meeting date, meeting link, and options to reschedule or cancel the meeting. At the same time, the sales team receives a notification in Slack and the meeting activity is saved in HubSpot notes.

## Step 7 : Send Reminder Emails Before Meeting

This step automatically sends reminder emails before the meeting starts. The first reminder is sent 24 hours before the meeting, and the second reminder is sent 1 hour before the meeting. These reminders help the customer remember the meeting and quickly join the call on time.
